title: (No AI) Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
author: Elizabeth Lopatto
content:
![Graphic photo collage of Sam Altman and Elon Musk.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/268474_musk_vs_altman_CVirginia.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Sam Altman and Elon Musk are set to face off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of tech’s leading AI startup, OpenAI. In [2024, Musk filed a lawsuit](https://www.theverge.com/2024/3/1/24087473/elon-musk-openai-lawsuit-nonprofit-mission) accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. The trial began with jury selection on April 27th. Today, Elon Musk has arrived at the courthouse as the opening arguments began for both sides.

Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and cofounder Greg Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, [OpenAI says](https://x.com/OpenAINewsroom/status/2048776645142872368?s=20) that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.

In his lawsuit, [Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/917755/musk-altman-openai-xai-gossip), and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.

_Here’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:_

*   [“Musk was furious that OpenAI succeeded.”](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919730/musk-was-furious-that-openai-succeeded)
*   [OpenAI: Musk’s lawsuit is a “pageant of hypocrisy.”](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919724/openai-mr-musks-lawsuit-is-a-pageant-of-hypocrisy)
*   [Sam Altman’s “related party conflicted transactions” are how he made money on OpenAI, Molo says.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919669/sam-altmans-related-party-conflicted-transactions-are-how-he-made-money-on-openai-molo-says)
*   [Technical difficulties.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919654/technical-difficulties)
*   [OpenAI is like a museum store that has looted the Picassos and pocketed the profits.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919649/openai-is-like-a-museum-store-that-has-looted-the-picassos-and-pocketed-the-profits)
*   [AGI might be out of fashion in the AI world, but it will be at the center of this trial](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919630/agi-might-be-out-of-fashion-in-the-ai-world-but-it-will-be-at-the-center-of-this-trial)
*   [“The defendants in this case stole a charity.”](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919626/the-defendants-in-this-case-stole-a-charity)
*   [Musk and Altman go to court](https://www.theverge.com/podcast/919534/musk-openai-trial-vergecast)
*   [Good morning from the Musk v Altman line outside the courtroom.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919559/good-morning-from-the-musk-v-altman-line-outside-the-courtroom)
*   [Jury selection in Musk v. Altman: ‘People don’t like him’](https://www.theverge.com/tech/919469/elon-musk-dont-like)
*   [We have a jury.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919424/we-have-a-jury)
*   [Elon Musk’s lawyer tried to get some jurors thrown out for disliking Musk.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919413/elon-musks-lawyer-tried-to-get-some-jurors-thrown-out-for-disliking-musk)
*   [Apparently things are exciting outside.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919388/apparently-things-are-exciting-outside)
*   [We have gone through the first 20 potential jurors.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919339/we-have-gone-through-the-first-20-potential-jurors)
*   [Voir dire has begun.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919251/voir-dire-has-begun)
*   [The Elon Musk vs. OpenAI trial starts today.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919149/trial-starts-today)
*   [Elon Musk drops fraud claims against OpenAI and Sam Altman before trial.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/918909/elon-musk-drops-fraud-claims-against-openai-and-sam-altman-before-trial)
*   [Musk vs. Altman is here, and it’s going to get messy](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/917755/musk-altman-openai-xai-gossip)
*   [Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!](https://www.theverge.com/tech/906027/elon-musk-lawsuit-ipo-spacex-tesla)
*   [‘Sideshow’ concerns and billionaire dreams: What I learned from Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/column/863319/highlights-musk-v-altman-openai)
*   [Elon Musk’s xAI is suing OpenAI and Apple](https://www.theverge.com/news/765171/elon-musk-apple-openai-antitrust-lawsuit)
*   [Inside Elon Musk’s messy breakup with OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/2024/11/18/24299787/elon-musk-openai-lawsuit-sam-altman-xai-google-deepmind)
*   [Elon Musk is suing OpenAI and Sam Altman again](https://www.theverge.com/2024/8/5/24213557/elon-musk-openai-lawsuit-sam-altman-greg-brockman-revived)

In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. The trial began with jury selection on April 27th. Today, Elon Musk has arrived at the courthouse as the opening arguments began for both sides.
Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and cofounder Greg Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.

title: (No AI) Claude can now plug directly into Photoshop, Blender, and Ableton
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![Examples of Anthropic’s Claude connector for the Blender 3D modelling software.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/Claude-creative-connectors-Blender-.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Claude’s new Blender connector lets you debug scenes, build new tools, and batch-apply object changes directly from the chatbot interface. | Image: Anthropic

Anthropic has launched a set of connectors for Claude that allow the AI chatbot to tap into popular creative software, including Adobe's Creative Cloud apps, Affinity, Blender, Ableton, Autodesk, and more.

This marks the company's latest efforts to break into the creative industry following its launch of [Claude Design](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/913963/anthropic-launched-a-new-design-product) earlier this month. The new connectors - which [enable Claude to access apps](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/917871/anthropic-claude-personal-app-connectors), retrieve data, and take actions within connected services - are "designed to make it easier to use Claude for creative work," according to Anthropic, and can be used for specific functions in each app.

The Adobe for creativity connector can draw fr …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919648/anthropic-claude-creative-connectors-adobe-blender)

title: (No AI) The Race Is on to Keep AI Agents From Running Wild With Your Credit Cards
dc:creator: Lily Hay Newman
content: AI agents may soon be buying your stuff for you. The FIDO Alliance has teamed up with Google and Mastercard to try to ensure that shopping in the near future isn't a complete disaster.
contentSnippet: AI agents may soon be buying your stuff for you. The FIDO Alliance has teamed up with Google and Mastercard to try to ensure that shopping in the near future isn't a complete disaster.
